objective: Qualify one exact local model as 'qualified' for the website_audit.lighthouse_handoff track with priority_helper role, using the Benchmark Lab workflow.

pilot.reason: First prerequisite (qualified model capability for a companion track) now met. Second prerequisite (sufficient shadow routing evidence, min 3-5 comparisons) not yet accumulated.
requiredForActivation: ✅ Companion track with model role having 'qualified' qualification record — LFM2.5-1.2B-Thinking qualified for website_audit.lighthouse_handoff/priority_helper (score 0.9167)
❌ At least 3-5 shadow routing comparisons showing consistent recommendation agreement — not yet accumulated
Qualification record must be current (not expired or stale)
Runtime availability and model readiness verifiable

humanReview.failureReason: INVENTED_AUDIT: model produced auditId 'unknown' for 'Multiple competing high-priority issues' scenario (12 high-severity audits). Model defaulted to generic invented response instead of selecting from real audits.
humanReview.acceptanceRationale: 11/12 PASS (91.7%) reliable for typical Lighthouse reports (5-8 findings). Failed case is hardest scenario — 12 competing audits exceeds model's reasoning ceiling. No invented facts in any passing case. Minor guardrail gaps acceptable for v1 qualification.
knownLimitations: Input complexity ceiling ~8-10 high-severity audits before degradation
Guardrail compliance inconsistent — platform/stack constraints often omitted
Actionability scores lower (0.5) on cases needing specific implementation steps

commands: npm.cmd run qualification:generate -- --model lfm25-1p2b-thinking-local --evidence lfm25-1p2b-thinking-lighthouse-priority-v1 --role priority_helper --status qualified --role-status qualified
results: Benchmark Lighthouse Priority mock test: 12/12 PASS
Qualification record generated: status 'qualified', role 'priority_helper', track 'website_audit.lighthouse_handoff', score 0.9167
Human review: approved-with-limitations by JP Pannell — failed case lh-priority-011 documented
Local Brain /health reports qualified capability for website_audit.lighthouse_handoff/priority_helper (2 qualified capabilities total, up from 1)

passed: Custom benchmark track created at benchmark-lab/locaily/tracks/lighthouse-priority-helper/ with suite.json, output.schema.json, evaluation-rubric.json, mock-responses.json
12 scenario cases covering core and edge Lighthouse priority behaviors
Mock test passes 12/12 without Ollama dependency
Custom runner and CLI work independently of generic suite-runner
All 3 live candidate models evaluated against real Ollama (llama3.2, LFM2.5-1.2B-Thinking, LFM2.5-350M)
Model card generated from promoted evidence
Local Brain consumes qualification record and reports correct state
Non-candidate models with lower performance do not produce misleading qualification records
No existing evidence, qualification records, or checksums modified
All existing test suites pass without regression
